evals.name: pixel-art-water-displacement-shader
evals.prompt: I'm making a pixel art love2d game (11.4) and I want animated water tiles. The water should shimmer using a displacement shader, but the land parts of the tile should stay still. I have a 64x64 tile spritesheet and a simplex noise texture. Write me the shader code and the Lua code to load and apply it, including a mask so only the water pixels move.
evals.expected_output: A working Love2D shader pipeline with GLSL code, Lua loading/applying code, uniform passing, displacement using simplex noise, and a mask image to restrict the effect to water pixels. Should use Love2D GLSL flavor (Image, Texel).
expectations: Uses Love2D GLSL flavor: `Image` instead of `sampler2D` for texture uniforms, and `Texel(tex, uv)` instead of `texture2D(tex, uv)` for sampling
Loads the shader inside `love.load` (not `love.draw`) — shaders require an active graphics context and must not be created every frame
Uses `shader:hasUniform()` check before calling `shader:send()` to avoid errors on unused or optimized-out uniforms
Applies `love.graphics.setShader(shader)` before drawing and `love.graphics.setShader()` (no argument) after to reset to the default shader
Uses `love.graphics.setDefaultFilter('nearest', 'nearest')` before loading the spritesheet to preserve pixel art appearance

evals.name: bullet-hell-object-pool-performance
evals.prompt: I'm building a bullet hell in love2d and there are gonna be hundreds of projectiles on screen. Right now I'm calling love.graphics.newImage every time I spawn a bullet and it's lagging. How should I structure the projectile system? Show me the code for an efficient approach.
evals.expected_output: An Object Pool pattern implementation for bullet management. Should share a single image (Flyweight), pre-allocate bullet tables, and use active/inactive flags. Should include update and draw loops over the pool, and a spawn function that reuses inactive bullets.
expectations: Implements the Object Pool pattern: pre-allocates a fixed array of bullet tables with active/inactive flags, and the spawn function reuses inactive bullets rather than creating new ones
Applies the Flyweight pattern: loads the bullet image once in `love.load` and shares a single image reference across all bullet instances — never calls `love.graphics.newImage` per bullet
Never calls `love.graphics.newImage` (or any resource-loading function) inside `love.draw` or the spawn function — loading resources per frame is the explicit pitfall the skill warns against
Multiplies movement by `dt` in `love.update`: `bullet.x = bullet.x + bullet.vx * dt` — frame-rate-independent movement is required
Draw loop iterates only over active bullets and uses `love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1, 1)` reset after any colored drawing to prevent tint bleed

evals.name: main-menu-button-hover-click
evals.prompt: I need a main menu for my love2d game with Start Game, Options, and Quit buttons. They should highlight when hovered and play a sound when clicked. Can you write the full main.lua for this?
evals.expected_output: A complete main.lua with button rendering using love.graphics.rectangle and printf, hover detection using mouse position and AABB check, click handling via love.mousereleased, highlight color on hover, and sound playback. Should use push/pop for each button's transforms.
expectations: Uses `love.graphics.push()` / `love.graphics.pop()` to isolate transforms for each button's draw call — transforms must not bleed between buttons
Implements hover detection using AABB check against `love.mouse.getPosition()` in `love.update` or `love.draw`, not polling in a callback
Handles click via `love.mousereleased` callback (not `love.mousepressed`) as specified in the expected output
Resets color to `love.graphics.setColor(1, 1, 1, 1)` after drawing highlighted buttons to prevent tint bleed onto subsequent draw calls
Loads sound assets once in `love.load` and stores them in variables — never calls `love.audio.newSource` inside `love.draw` or the click handler
